mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A restrictive federal funds rate above 4.5% elevates the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and increases debt service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Effective federal funds rate at 5.02% as of Aug 2007
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in industrial production and rising unemployment creates a joint real-economy contraction signal that amplifies downside equity risk beyond either indicator alone, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of month-over-month INDPRO decline and UNRATE increase do not produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than either variable alone (interaction term t-statistic > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal tightening credit conditions and reduced construction activity that spill over into broader consumption and employment, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1381 as of Jul 2007, down from cycle peak
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle periods with a Rank IC below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in advance retail sales signals robust consumer spending that supports near-term revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month positive momentum does not coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of accelerating inflation and a high federal funds rate creates a stagflationary discount where rising input costs coincide with restrictive policy, compressing profit margins and equity valuations simultaneously over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (p > 0.05 in pooled regression).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rising above 5% triggers rapid downward revision of consumption forecasts and corporate earnings estimates, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of Aug 2007, near cycle low
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not negative in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth signals aggregate output expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings over the subsequent quarter, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to achieve a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.
